topic: Honest adaptive inference for the optimal treatment-policy value across near-tie and exceptional-law boundaries: derive local experiments over unknown margin exponent/constant, tie-atom mass, and nuisance-smoothness strata; construct one cross-fitted data-adaptive interval uniformly honest over the declared union; characterize and attain the minimax expected-length adaptation diagram, with matching causal lower bounds and Liu-Zhang-Nie-Soon's rilpivirine-versus-efavirenz individualized-regime analysis as consumer

open_problems.open_problem: What is the minimax expected-length diagram for confidence intervals for the binary optimal-policy value over classes indexed jointly by margin exponent and constant, tie-atom mass, and nuisance-function smoothness?

source_refs.claim: Adaptive smoothing gives root-n inference under a margin condition and a polynomial gap between contrast-estimation error and the admissible smoothing range, but optimality is only within a robust asymptotically linear unbiased class.
source_refs.author: Shuoxun Xu and Xinzhou Guo

source_refs.claim: The paper characterizes pathwise differentiability and constructs root-n intervals at exceptional laws under sufficient value-regret and nuisance conditions tied to a margin exponent.
source_refs.author: Alexander R. Luedtke and Mark J. van der Laan

source_refs.claim: Subagging yields nominal-coverage intervals under nonunique regimes and improves MSE over simple sample splitting, but does not derive minimax expected-length rates across margin and nuisance strata.
source_refs.author: Chengchun Shi, Wenbin Lu, and Rui Song

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing optimal-value results provide root-n sufficient conditions at fixed laws or efficiency comparisons inside a restricted estimator class. They do not identify the testing modulus when the margin exponent and constant, mass at exactly zero contrast, and nuisance smoothness vary jointly. The literature therefore does not determine where root-n length ceases to be possible, what slower rate replaces it when the nuisance-margin gap closes, or whether tie mass changes only constants or also rates.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem giving matching upper and causal lower bounds for minimax expected interval length on every margin-by-tie-mass-by-smoothness stratum, including explicit phase boundaries and least-favorable submodels.

writing_exemplar.why: It provides the closest modern motivation arc from optimal-regime evaluation through nonregularity to an explicit efficiency question.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: optimal regimes require value inference -> ties destroy classical pathwise-differentiable efficiency -> adaptive smoothing restores root-n inference under a rate gap -> determine the full minimax length diagram when the gap and tie mass vary

method_exemplars.technique: Use between-class and ordered moduli of continuity to prove expected-length lower bounds and construct constant-factor adaptive confidence intervals.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired object is exactly an expected-length adaptation diagram over nonnested strata, with the causal local experiment replacing Gaussian white noise.

method_exemplars.technique: Build margin-indexed plug-in upper bounds and matching Assouad-style lower bounds using localized alternatives near the decision boundary.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Optimal-value regret is controlled by the same small-contrast region as classification excess risk, so its margin constructions supply candidate least-favorable causal submodels.

method_exemplars.technique: Embed a nondifferentiable target in differentiable parametric submodels and derive local impossibility results that motivate local asymptotic minimax criteria.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The optimal value is a max-type nondifferentiable functional at ties, precisely the setting where ordinary regular-efficiency lower bounds fail.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the Le Cam local experiment for the optimal-policy value along sequences that move between a near tie and an exceptional law, allowing both local contrast drift and tie-atom mass to vary with sample size?

source_refs.claim: For nondifferentiable functionals, locally asymptotically unbiased and quantile-unbiased estimators generally do not exist, motivating local asymptotic minimaxity and one-sided criteria.
source_refs.author: Keisuke Hirano and Jack R. Porter

source_refs.claim: The optimal value is generally not pathwise differentiable when the blip is zero on a positive-probability set with residual outcome variation.
source_refs.author: Alexander R. Luedtke and Mark J. van der Laan

source_refs.claim: A variance bound is derived for a robust asymptotically linear unbiased estimator class at regular and nonregular laws, while unrestricted asymptotically linear estimators are acknowledged to be too broad for that bound.
source_refs.author: Shuoxun Xu and Xinzhou Guo

open_problems.why_unsolved: Fixed-law pathwise differentiability separates regular from exceptional laws, while recent adaptive-smoothing theory supplies a fixed-law variance comparison. Neither determines the limit experiment under contiguous sequences where a treatment contrast approaches zero and an atomic tie subgroup appears, disappears, or changes mass. Without that experiment, the minimax risk and honest-interval lower bound outside a chosen asymptotically linear estimator class remain unknown.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A LAN, LAQ, or explicitly non-Gaussian limit-experiment theorem indexed by local contrast drift and tie-atom mass, together with its minimax confidence-length bound and an attaining procedure.

writing_exemplar.why: Its setup cleanly isolates the exceptional set, explains why the optimal value loses pathwise differentiability, and motivates inference that survives nonuniqueness.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: optimal value is smooth when the optimal action is locally stable -> positive-mass ties make the max functional nondifferentiable -> fixed-law root-n intervals can still be constructed -> characterize the contiguous experiment governing transitions into and out of ties

method_exemplars.technique: Analyze differentiable parametric submodels passing through a kink and use local unbiasedness impossibility to identify the appropriate minimax criterion.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The exceptional-law boundary is a causal max-functional kink, so the same local-submodel argument identifies which Gaussian-shift decisions are feasible.

method_exemplars.technique: Estimate the directional derivative of a max-type map and bootstrap the resulting directional delta-method limit rather than the original nonsmooth plug-in statistic.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The local experiment must encode the directional derivative of the conditional maximum, and this supplies the natural inference machinery once that derivative is characterized.

open_problems.open_problem: Can one cross-fitted, fully data-adaptive confidence interval be uniformly honest over a declared union containing unknown margin exponents and constants, unknown nuisance smoothness, vanishing near-tie regions, and positive tie atoms while attaining each stratum's optimal expected length whenever adaptation is information-theoretically possible?

source_refs.claim: The proposed cross-fitted tuning rule does not require the numerical margin exponent or nuisance convergence rate, but validity requires a polynomial gap between lower and upper smoothing scales.
source_refs.author: Shuoxun Xu and Xinzhou Guo

source_refs.claim: Softmax smoothing gives confidence intervals for optimal policy values with a constant number of nuisance fits and is efficient when treatment nonresponse has zero probability.
source_refs.author: Justin Whitehouse, Qizhao Chen, Morgane Austern, and Vasilis Syrgkanis

source_refs.claim: For convex smoothness classes, honest nonparametric intervals generally cannot adapt to an unknown smoothness bound and must account explicitly for worst-case bias.
source_refs.author: Timothy B. Armstrong and Michal Kolesár

open_problems.why_unsolved: Current causal procedures establish asymptotic normality under fixed margin and nuisance-rate conditions, not uniform coverage over a union whose boundary includes closing smoothing-scale gaps and changing atomic mass. General honest-CI theory warns that adaptation over unknown smoothness can be impossible without separation or self-similarity. The unresolved issue is which causal margin and tie restrictions make cross-fitted adaptation possible and how to preserve coverage exactly where data-driven smoothing selection is hardest.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: Either a single cross-fitted interval with a theorem proving uniform coverage and stratum-optimal expected length over an explicitly stated union, or a sharp impossibility theorem plus minimal separation or self-similarity conditions under which such an interval exists.

writing_exemplar.why: It already presents the closest cross-fitted adaptive-smoothing construction and clearly identifies the lower-versus-upper smoothing-scale gap.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: plug-in cross-fitting is efficient away from ties -> smoothing is needed at exceptional laws -> data-driven tuning works when an admissible scale gap exists -> obtain uniform honesty and optimal length when that gap and all strata are unknown

method_exemplars.technique: Construct bias-aware critical values and bandwidths from worst-case bias, then compare interval efficiency by exact modulus calculations.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A uniformly honest causal interval must explicitly pay for smoothing and nuisance bias rather than rely only on pointwise remainder negligibility.

method_exemplars.technique: Use ordered between-class moduli both to rule out impossible adaptation and to construct adaptive intervals when the modulus permits it.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The declared causal union is a collection of strata, so its attainable adaptation pattern should be governed by a causal analogue of the between-class modulus.

method_exemplars.technique: Cross-fit nuisance functions, locally smooth only near the estimated treatment boundary, and choose the smoothing scale from a split-sample approximation-error statistic.
method_exemplars.why_closest: This is the most direct estimator construction to extend from fixed-condition validity to uniform coverage over the proposed union.

open_problems.open_problem: For finitely many treatments, how do arbitrary partial-tie patterns among the best actions alter the efficiency bound and honest confidence length for the unrestricted optimal-policy value?

source_refs.claim: The efficiency-optimal adaptive-smoothing theory is developed for binary treatment, and the discussion explicitly limits the main analysis to binary optimal treatment regimes.
source_refs.author: Shuoxun Xu and Xinzhou Guo

source_refs.claim: Softmax smoothing covers general maximum-of-score functionals, but reports semiparametric efficiency for optimal policy value only when treatment nonresponse has zero probability.
source_refs.author: Justin Whitehouse, Qizhao Chen, Morgane Austern, and Vasilis Syrgkanis

source_refs.claim: Multi-action observational policy learning has asymptotically minimax-optimal regret using augmented inverse-propensity scores, but its target is policy learning rather than honest inference for the unrestricted optimal value at ties.
source_refs.author: Zhengyuan Zhou, Susan Athey, and Stefan Wager

open_problems.why_unsolved: With more than two actions, exceptional laws are not described by one zero contrast: different covariate strata can have different tied subsets, and several pairwise gaps may approach zero at different rates. General softmax methods handle a maximum computationally, while multi-action policy-learning theory handles regret, but neither supplies a tie-pattern-specific efficiency or minimax expected-length characterization for causal value inference.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-action theorem expressing the local limit law and optimal confidence length in terms of the active tied-action sets and pairwise margin geometry, plus a cross-fitted estimator attaining the bound uniformly over specified partial-tie regimes.

writing_exemplar.why: Its maximum-of-nuisance-scores formulation is the cleanest narrative bridge from optimal policy value to a finite-action max functional.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: optimal value is a maximum of causal scores -> maxima are nondifferentiable at action ties -> softmax smoothing enables feasible inference -> characterize efficiency and honest length for arbitrary finite-action partial ties

method_exemplars.technique: Apply a directional delta method and derivative-aware bootstrap to a max map whose derivative takes the maximum over the active coordinates.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The active-coordinate set is exactly the tied-action set, so the proof machinery naturally records changing partial-tie geometry.

method_exemplars.technique: Construct multi-action augmented inverse-propensity scores with cross-fitted nuisances and prove uniform policy-value control.
method_exemplars.why_closest: These scores provide the causal Gaussian coordinates whose maximum and tie geometry must enter the desired local experiment.

method_exemplars.technique: Choose asymmetric local smoothing weights by a variance-minimizing variational argument under nonregularity.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The binary variance decomposition suggests a simplex-valued multi-action weight optimization over each tied-action face.

open_problems.open_problem: Can optimized rilpivirine-versus-efavirenz regime values be equipped with uniformly honest intervals under monotone censoring or missingness when biomarker strata may contain near ties or exact treatment-response ties?

source_refs.claim: The case study uses G-computation, IPW, and augmented IPW under monotone coarsening, then cross-validation to remove resubstitution bias when evaluating optimized regimes.
source_refs.author: Wei Liu, Zhiwei Zhang, Lei Nie, and Guoxing Soon
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Root-n inference at exceptional laws is established for the single-time optimal mean under standard observed-data treatment models, conditional on nuisance and value-regret requirements.
source_refs.author: Alexander R. Luedtke and Mark J. van der Laan

open_problems.why_unsolved: Liu et al. correct optimization resubstitution bias and handle monotone coarsening for fixed-regime evaluation, but do not provide a coverage theorem uniform across biomarker-specific near ties and exceptional laws after optimizing the regime. Exceptional-law methods, in turn, do not directly account for the additional missingness and censoring nuisances and the cross-validated optimization workflow used in this HIV analysis.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A cross-fitted augmented-IPW or targeted estimator with an explicit influence representation, a uniform-coverage theorem over coarsening and tie-margin strata, and a resulting confidence interval for the optimized virologic-response value in the rilpivirine-versus-efavirenz analysis.

writing_exemplar.why: It supplies the exact clinical motivation, biomarker-based policy formulation, monotone-coarsening setup, and optimized-regime consumer analysis.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: rilpivirine and efavirenz efficacy may vary with viral load and CD4 -> individualized regimes are optimized using coarsened trial outcomes -> cross-validation removes resubstitution bias -> obtain honest uncertainty for the optimized value when biomarker strata are near-tied or tied

method_exemplars.technique: Stabilize one-step influence-function contributions online and control the estimated-rule value regret at exceptional laws.
method_exemplars.why_closest: This directly addresses the nonregular optimal-value component that must be combined with the coarsening influence function.

method_exemplars.technique: Use cross-validated targeted minimum-loss estimation to separate rule fitting from value evaluation and remove restrictive empirical-process conditions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its foldwise targeting architecture matches Liu et al.'s cross-validated optimized-regime evaluation and can incorporate censoring and missingness nuisances.

method_exemplars.technique: Locally smooth the estimated treatment decision near zero contrast and select smoothing from split-sample approximation error.
method_exemplars.why_closest: This is the most direct route to making the HIV value interval insensitive to unknown near-tie versus tie status.

literature_map:
1. Luedtke and van der Laan (2016, Annals of Statistics, DOI:10.1214/15-AOS1384) characterize pathwise differentiability of the optimal value and construct root-n intervals at exceptional laws.
2. Shi, Lu, and Song (2020, JMLR 21(176)) use subagging for nonregular optimal-value inference and compare MSE and interval length with sample splitting and the online estimator.
3. Xu and Guo (2026 revision, arXiv:2509.09773) add cross-fitted local adaptive smoothing and a variance bound within a robust asymptotically linear unbiased estimator class.
4. Whitehouse, Chen, Austern, and Syrgkanis (2025/2026, arXiv:2507.11780) study constant-refit softmax inference for optimal policy values and general maximum-of-score functionals.
5. Chen, Austern, and Syrgkanis (2023, arXiv:2303.04416) derive softmax-based inference for optimal dynamic policies using triangular-array CLTs and smoothing-bias control.
6. Hirano and Porter (2012, Econometrica, DOI:10.3982/ECTA8681) prove impossibility of locally unbiased regular inference for nondifferentiable functionals and point toward local asymptotic minimax criteria.
7. Fang and Santos (2019, Review of Economic Studies, DOI:10.1093/restud/rdy049) provide directional-delta and derivative-aware bootstrap machinery for max-type nonsmooth maps.
8. Audibert and Tsybakov (2007, arXiv:0708.2321) give margin-indexed fast plug-in rates and matching minimax lower bounds, the closest source for near-decision-boundary constructions.
9. Athey and Wager (2021, Econometrica, arXiv:1702.02896) connect doubly robust policy scores to semiparametric efficiency and minimax regret for observational policy learning.
10. Zhou, Athey, and Wager (2023, Operations Research, DOI:10.1287/opre.2022.2271) extend minimax-regret policy learning to multiple actions, but do not solve honest optimal-value inference at partial ties.
11. Cai and Low (2004, Annals of Statistics, DOI:10.1214/009053604000000049) develop ordered-modulus lower bounds and constructions for expected-length adaptation across parameter classes.
12. Low (1997, Annals of Statistics, DOI:10.1214/aos/1030741084) and Armstrong and Kolesár (2020, Quantitative Economics, DOI:10.3982/QE1199) show why honest smoothness adaptation generally requires worst-case bias accounting or added structure.
13. Chambaz, Zheng, and van der Laan (2017, Annals of Statistics, DOI:10.1214/16-AOS1534) obtain targeted sequential inference under nonexceptionality and a companion margin condition.
14. Liu, Zhang, Nie, and Soon (2017, JASA, DOI:10.1080/01621459.2017.1280404) optimize rilpivirine-versus-efavirenz regimes using G-computation, IPW, and AIPW under monotone coarsening and cross-validation, but leave uniform exceptional-law uncertainty unresolved.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined the active qid; it contains only initialization/state records and no prior proposal or reviewer artifacts.
Prioritized the adjacent accepted stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap/v1 entry; its Honest scope leaves a strict feasible-versus-converse minimax gap under margin and weak overlap.
Mined failed stat_prte_ot_boundary_adaptation/v1, including its bank README, final writeup, four rejected-angle files, and nine reviewer JSONs; it yielded the strongest generic honest-adaptation signals.
That PRTE entry records both a refuted cross-alpha two-point construction and two reraisable problems: modified adaptation classes and a primitive fixed-alpha calibrator.
Mined downgraded eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier/v1 and its reviewer trail; the bank README identifies the missing primitive maximal-inequality plus local-minimax derivation.
Mined accepted stat_dose_response_minimax/holder_anisotropic_converse; its README preserves the unrestricted same-class smoothness frontier as an open handle.
No same-topic upgrade parent was supplied, and no prior CausalSmith artifact directly studies optimal-policy-value inference at tie atoms or exceptional laws; consequently no Track-A candidate was cross-linked or retained in the topic-specific filtered ledger.
